claim_restated: The subjective-dimension metadata pipeline has a circular, multi-home source of truth that violates the documented base/ architecture contract: (1) base/subjective_dimensions.py imports upward into intelligence/ and languages/ (lines 10-17), violating the README.md:95 'zero upward imports' rule, (2) metadata_legacy.py imports DISPLAY_NAMES from engine/_scoring/subjective/core (line 5), creating intelligenceâ†’engine dependency, (3) scoring core reaches back into intelligence/review/dimensions/metadata via runtime cycle-break imports (core.py:61-80), (4) DISPLAY_NAMES dict is duplicated identically across base/subjective_dimensions.py:21-45 and engine/_scoring/subjective/core.py:9-33, with equivalent defaults in metadata_legacy.py:9-38.
why_poor_engineering: The circular dependency chain baseâ†’intelligenceâ†’engineâ†’(runtime)intelligence is a real architectural violation of a documented contract (README.md:95). The identical DISPLAY_NAMES duplication across 2 files (with equivalent data in a 3rd) creates a multi-home source of truth that can silently diverge. The cycle-break runtime imports with broad except clauses mask real errors.

why_missed.what_should_catch: An import-graph linter that enforces the documented layering rule (base/ has zero upward imports) and a duplicate-constant detector that flags identical dict literals across files.
why_missed.why_not_caught: The circular dependency is mitigated by runtime imports with fallbacks, so the system works correctly in practice. The violation was known (acknowledged by cycle-break comments) and accepted as an architectural shortcut.
why_missed.what_could_catch: A CI check using import-linter or similar tool enforcing the documented baseâ†’engineâ†’intelligenceâ†’app layer ordering, plus a deduplication pass consolidating DISPLAY_NAMES to a single canonical location.

claims.title: base/subjective_dimensions.py upward imports violate architecture contract
claims.detail: base/subjective_dimensions.py:10-17 imports from desloppify.intelligence.review.dimensions.data, desloppify.intelligence.review.dimensions.metadata, and desloppify.languages. README.md:95 states 'base/ has zero upward imports'. Partially covered by S307 (which noted it as 'known architectural shortcut').

claims.title: metadata_legacy.py pulls DISPLAY_NAMES from scoring core
claims.detail: metadata_legacy.py:5: 'from desloppify.engine._scoring.subjective.core import DISPLAY_NAMES'. This creates intelligenceâ†’engine dependency.

claims.title: Scoring core reaches back into metadata via cycle-break imports
claims.detail: engine/_scoring/subjective/core.py:61-69 (_dimension_display_name) and 72-80 (_dimension_weight) use runtime imports from desloppify.intelligence.review.dimensions.metadata with explicit '# cycle-break' comments. Partially covered by S312 (which noted the _dimension_weight 1.0 fallback).

claims.title: Duplicated dimension defaults across files
claims.detail: DISPLAY_NAMES dict (20 entries, identical) exists in both base/subjective_dimensions.py:21-45 and engine/_scoring/subjective/core.py:9-33. metadata_legacy.py:9-38 has equivalent weight and reset-on-scan defaults also duplicated in base/subjective_dimensions.py:49-78.

fix_description: No fix needed â€” verdict is YES_WITH_CAVEATS. The issue is real but partially covered by prior submissions and mitigated by existing cycle-break patterns.
notes: All 4 claims are technically accurate and well-evidenced. However, individual components overlap with S307 (upward imports from base/) and S312 (_dimension_weight fallback). S005's added value is mapping the complete circular chain and identifying the multi-home DISPLAY_NAMES duplication as a systemic pattern. Practical impact is low because the cycle-break runtime imports with fallbacks prevent actual runtime failures.
